concept: The interior angles of a triangle always sum to 180°, regardless of its shape.
why: You'll use this in any proof involving triangles — it's the building block of Euclidean geometry.
common_mistakes: Forgetting that the 180° sum holds only in plane (Euclidean) geometry — on a sphere, angles sum to more than 180°.
Confusing interior and exterior angles — exterior angles sum to 360°.
generate_task: If two angles of a triangle are 45° and 85°, what is the third?

quiz.question: The interior angles of any triangle sum to...
quiz.explanation: This is the fundamental theorem of plane geometry for triangles.

quiz.question: Exterior angles of any triangle sum to ___°.
Does the 180° rule hold on a sphere?
quiz.answer: no, spherical triangles sum to more than 180°
quiz.explanation: On a sphere, geodesic triangles have an angle excess proportional to their area.
